title: Koffeechat: Why We're Betting Everything on Conversation
excerpt: Networking tools optimize for contacts. Koffeechat optimizes for conversations. Here's the thinking behind our flagship product.
content:
Ask anyone how they got their best job, met their co-founder, or found their mentor, and you'll almost never hear "a job board" or "a connection request." You'll hear a story about a conversation — usually one they almost didn't have.

The problem with networking

Modern networking optimizes for the wrong unit. Contacts. Followers. Connections. All nouns, all countable, all nearly worthless on their own. The thing that actually moves lives is a verb: talking.

But real conversations are expensive to arrange. Who do I reach out to? What do I say? When are we both free? Is thirty minutes too much to ask? Every step leaks people, and the ones it leaks most are exactly the people with the smallest networks to begin with.

What Koffeechat does differently

  • It matches on curiosity, not clout. You tell Koffeechat what you do and what you want to learn. It finds someone worth meeting — not someone with the most followers.

  • It keeps chats short. Every conversation is capped at the length of a coffee. Low stakes, easy yes.

  • It handles the logistics. Matching, mutual opt-in, scheduling — all automatic. You just show up.

Where it's headed

We're rolling Koffeechat out community by community — companies pairing employees across teams, schools connecting alumni with students, communities keeping their members close. If you run a community like that, we'd love to brew something together.
author: Alttene Team
